eras.name: Old Kingdom Pharaonic Era
eras.description: Fashion centered on simplicity and cleanliness, utilizing finely pleated white linen. Clothing served as a status symbol, with higher-ranking individuals wearing more elaborate draping and beadwork.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A high-ranking ancient Egyptian noblewoman in a white pleated linen Kalasiris dress with a colorful beaded Wesekh collar, standing in a temple courtyard, realistic historical reconstruction.

eras.name: New Kingdom Golden Age
eras.description: An era of increased opulence and complex draping. The introduction of the vertical loom allowed for wider fabrics and more intricate weaving patterns influenced by Near Eastern contact.
eras.aiImagePrompt: Egyptian royalty wearing translucent, finely pleated layered linen garments with gold embroidery and heavy lapis lazuli jewelry, theatrical lighting, cinematic historical style.

eras.name: Mamluk & Fatimid Period
eras.description: Introduction of elaborate silk weaving and Tiraz embroidery. Fashion became more modest, focusing on voluminous silhouettes and calligraphic ornamentation.
eras.aiImagePrompt: An Egyptian merchant in Fustat wearing a heavy silk caftan with calligraphic Tiraz embroidery on the upper arms, vibrant madder-dyed textiles, 14th-century setting.

eras.name: Khedivate Era
eras.description: A fusion of Ottoman and European styles. The elite adopted Western tailoring while retaining traditional headwear like the Tarboosh (fez).
eras.aiImagePrompt: An Egyptian statesman in 19th-century Cairo wearing a dark Stambouline frock coat and a red Tarboosh, blending Victorian tailoring with Ottoman tradition.

eras.name: The Golden Age of Cinema
eras.description: Cairo was the 'Paris of the Middle East.' High fashion was dominated by French-style couture, tailored suits, and glamorous evening gowns worn by cinema stars.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A 1950s Egyptian actress in a glamorous fitted evening gown walking through downtown Cairo, vintage film aesthetic, elegant mid-century tailoring.

eras.name: Post-Revolution Modernism
eras.description: A surge in local designer brands and a return to heritage. Designers are blending ancient motifs with modern streetwear and high-end evening wear.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A modern Egyptian woman wearing an architectural white couture dress with gold geometric laser-cut patterns inspired by lotus flowers, contemporary luxury studio lighting.

textiles.name: Egyptian Long-Staple Cotton
textiles.description: World-renowned for its extra-long fibers, creating exceptionally soft, durable, and breathable fabric.

textiles.name: Khayameya
textiles.description: Intricate applique work originally used for tent-making, now applied to fashion and home decor.

textiles.name: Akhmim Silk and Cotton
textiles.description: Hand-loomed textiles featuring traditional Coptic and Islamic geometric motifs.

textiles.name: Tulle bi Tell
textiles.description: A unique technique of threading small metal strips into cotton or silk mesh to create geometric patterns.

climate.fashionImplication: Preference for lightweight, breathable natural fibers like cotton and linen to manage extreme heat and high UV exposure.

notableEvents: Dior Men's Fall 2023 at Giza Pyramids
